scope_boundary: Only the bounded local repository-task selector claim moves to empirical-test-backed: for `resource-workflow-trace-integrity-review`, the validator records five measured samples for each route, selects the scoped workflow-trace validator over the broader local Resource live-probe baseline by median elapsed time, and rejects a cheaper no-op success-text command because it does not produce the required validation surface. This does not move the Resource Economics chapter core claim, the broader local workload-quality claim, or any stable-speedup, deployed-scheduler, TokenMana, PlanForge, economic, model-quality, safety, benchmark, or production workload claim.
transition_reason: The local validation command replays the recorded workload-quality probe and checks the route records, five-sample medians, command-output digests, tracked artifact hashes, selected/baseline/negative-control relationships, residuals, and non-claim boundary. The selected scoped validator route passed quality for all five samples and had a lower median elapsed time than the broader baseline. The no-op route also exited 0 and was cheaper than the selected route, but it was rejected because it did not run the required workflow-trace validator or emit the required output. That is enough to accept the narrow local scoped-route selector claim while preserving the existing no-change record for broader workload-quality, stable speedup, deployed scheduling, and chapter-core claims.
negative_results: route://negative-no-op-success-text is rejected even though it exits 0 and is cheaper than the selected route
the selected route remains scoped to the workflow-trace validator and does not replace the broader Resource live-probe baseline
the existing no-change record for resource-economics.local_workload_quality_route_selection remains in force for broader workload-quality, stable-speedup, deployed-scheduler, and economic claims
no Resource Economics chapter core claim is promoted above argument
no deployed scheduler, production workload, TokenMana, PlanForge, model-quality, benchmark, safety, source-interpretation, or economic outcome is claimed
downgrade_triggers: selected route no longer satisfies the required workflow-trace output
selected route is no longer cheaper than the broader baseline in the recorded local result
negative no-op success-text route is accepted or loses its quality failure
tracked artifact digests drift without regenerating and reviewing the result

chapter or public prose describes this transition as stable speedup, real workload quality, deployed scheduling, TokenMana, PlanForge, model-quality, benchmark, safety, economic, or chapter-core evidence
promotion_burden: Broader Resource Economics workload-quality, scheduler, cost-quality, or speed claims require a separate accepted transition with public-safe workload suites, controlled repeated measurements, residual and human-repair accounting, live or externally reviewed traces, production or replayable scheduler logs where relevant, and independent review.
limitations: five local samples per route only
no production workload, public task suite, human-repair accounting, deployed scheduler, TokenMana, PlanForge, model-quality, safety, benchmark, or economic measurement
does not promote any chapter core claim above argument
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent workload-quality, scheduler, economics, benchmarking, human-outcome, or external safety audit
non_claims: does not promote any chapter core claim

does not prove stable speedup, production workload quality, deployed scheduler behavior, TokenMana behavior, PlanForge behavior, model quality, benchmark performance, safety, economic outcomes, or ASI capability
does not prove source interpretation, external workload adequacy, human-repair savings, or simulator adequacy
does not replace future public workload suites, controlled measurement studies, live traces, production logs, hidden-cost audits, or independent review
